skill.name: tutorial-engineer
skill.description: Creates step-by-step tutorials and educational content from code. Transforms complex concepts into progressive learning experiences with hands-on examples. Use PROACTIVELY for onboarding guides, feature tutorials, or concept explanations.
skill.summary: Creates progressive technical tutorials with objectives, examples, exercises, and checkpoints.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and five weak cryptographic algorithm patterns. Review found no shell execution, cryptographic code, malware behavior, or prompt injection; all reported patterns are false positives from instructional Markdown text.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: External Command
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 26 tells the assistant to open a local resource file when detailed examples are needed. It does not run a shell command, invoke an external process, or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a Markdown reference to a resource file, not executable syntax. No command execution behavior is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 describes creating step-by-step tutorials and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption API, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain descriptive metadata. There is no code or cryptographic context to support the static finding.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 5 uses tutorial description text about hands-on examples. It does not reference or implement a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is educational copy only. No encryption, hashing, or signature logic appears on this line.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 28 defines the assistant role for tutorial engineering. It contains no cryptographic code and no instruction to bypass security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a normal skill role description. It does not contain executable logic or suspicious override language.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 32 lists pedagogical design as a skill area. It does not mention cryptography, security primitives, or unsafe algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static hit maps to ordinary prose in a numbered list. No security-relevant implementation exists at this location.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 50 introduces exercise design for tutorials. It contains no weak algorithm use or cryptographic guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a tutorial outline heading. The reviewed context contains no related code, imports, or security-sensitive behavior.

content.value_statement: Technical teams often struggle to turn complex code into clear learning material. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured tutorials with objectives, examples, exercises, and checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Defines learning objectives, prerequisites, and expected tutorial outcomes.
Breaks complex technical topics into progressive tutorial sections.
Designs hands-on exercises, checkpoints, and troubleshooting notes.
Suggests tutorial formats such as quick starts, deep dives, and workshops.
Structures Markdown tutorials with numbered sections and validation steps.
limitations: Does not run or verify tutorial code by itself.
Requires accurate source material, product details, or code context from the user.
Does not create interactive learning platforms or hosted labs.
May need subject matter review for highly specialized technical domains.

use_cases.title: Onboard New Developers
use_cases.description: Turn a repository feature into a guided tutorial with prerequisites, setup, and validation checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Teach a New API
use_cases.description: Create a hands-on guide that introduces API concepts before asking readers to build working examples.

use_cases.title: Improve Training Material
use_cases.description: Review an existing tutorial outline and add exercises, common errors, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Beginner Tutorial
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a topic and want a first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a beginner tutorial for [topic]. Include learning objectives, prerequisites, setup steps, guided practice, checkpoints, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Code Into a Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: You have code and need educational documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this code or feature description to create a tutorial. Explain each concept before use, add hands-on exercises, and include troubleshooting notes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Workshop Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a longer learning path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-part workshop for [audience] about [topic]. Sequence the lessons from basic to advanced and include practice tasks for each section.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Tutorial for Learning Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: You need advanced review and improvement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this tutorial for learning quality. Identify unclear assumptions, missing validation steps, weak exercises, and places where the difficulty increases too quickly.

output_examples.input: Create an onboarding tutorial for a new repository feature.
output_examples.output: A Markdown tutorial outline with goals, prerequisites, setup, a guided walkthrough, checkpoints, troubleshooting, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Convert an API reference page into a hands-on lesson.
output_examples.output: A lesson plan that introduces concepts in order, adds practice tasks, and explains expected results after each task.

output_examples.input: Improve an existing tutorial draft for beginners.
output_examples.output: A review with gaps, reordered sections, added validation points, and suggested exercises for safer learning progression.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, prior knowledge level, and desired final outcome.
Include real code or product context so examples stay accurate.
Ask for checkpoints and troubleshooting when learners must complete tasks independently.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a tutorial without defining the learner and goal.
Do not use it as a substitute for testing code examples.
Do not skip subject matter review for critical or regulated technical content.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured technical tutorials with objectives, prerequisites, examples, exercises, checkpoints, and next steps.

faq.question: Can it work from existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the relevant code or feature description, and it can turn that material into a learning sequence.

faq.question: Does it execute or validate code examples?
faq.answer: No. It can request validation steps, but a developer should still run and test all examples.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for beginner content?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes prerequisites, progressive disclosure, and frequent checkpoints for beginners.

faq.question: What input improves results?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, learning goal, product context, code samples, and expected final project.
